Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian confirms Iran's intelligence chief was killed in an Israeli strike last night.


Masoud Pezeshkian (@drpezeshkian)

ترور ناجوانمردانه همکاران عزیزم اسماعیل خطیب، علی لاریجانی و عزیز نصیرزاده در کنار بعضی از اعضای خانواده و تیم همراهشان داغدارمان کرد. 
شهادت دو عضو کابینه و دبیر شعام و سرداران نظامی و بسیجی را به مردم بزرگ ایران تسلیت می‌گویم. مطمئنم راهشان محکم‌تر از قبل ادامه خواهد داشت.

There will be stronger condemnation from many nations over the bombing of oil facilities than they offered for the bombing of school girls in Iran. And if/when Iran retaliates against oil infrastructure in the region, it will be portrayed as naked Iranian aggression
Israeli officials told Axios, "the strike was coordinated with and approved by the Trump administration." No official confirmation of this from the White House yet.

Tasnim quoting the IRGC saying Iran will soon strike energy facilities in the Persian Gulf: 

"These centers have become direct and legitimate targets and will be targeted in the coming hours... clear and repeated warnings were given to your rulers about entering this dangerous path and gambling with the fate of their nations."

The specific site bombed, South Pars, is part of the largest natural gas field in the world, which is shared by Iran and Qatar.
nitter.net/majedalansari/status/2…


د. ماجد محمد الأنصاري Dr. Majed Al Ansari (@majedalansari)

The Israeli targeting of facilities linked to Iran’s South Pars field, an extension of Qatar’s North Field, is a dangerous & irresponsible step amid the current military escalation in the region. 

Targeting energy infrastructure constitutes a threat to global energy securit

⚡️Israel bombed a large gas facility in southern Iran. Sources in Tel Aviv claimed this was coordinated with the US. The IRGC says it will respond in kind and issued a warning saying oil/gas facilities in Qatar, UAE and Saudi are legitimate targets.
